steps.title: Use the maximum value
steps.content: The graph of $y=-x^2+bx-676$ opens downward. For the equation to have no real solutions, the graph must stay below the x-axis, so its maximum value must be less than $0$.

steps.title: Find the boundary
steps.content:
The vertex occurs at $x=\frac{b}{2}$. Substitute that value:
$$-\left(\frac{b}{2}\right)^2+b\left(\frac{b}{2}\right)-676=\frac{b^2}{4}-676$$
No real solutions requires $\frac{b^2}{4}-676\lt0$, so $b^2\lt2704=52^2$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Because $b$ is a positive integer, $b\lt52$. The greatest possible value is 51.
